stateKeys.name: authorizedAt
stateKeys.type: boolean — ABSTRACTION of authorized_at; the code tests presence only

stateKeys.name: hasPayment
stateKeys.type: boolean — ABSTRACTION of a linked Payment row; the code tests presence only

specialRules.name: already-authorized
specialRules.note: authorizePaymentSession short-circuits when a linked Payment exists AND authorized_at is set. Note it keys on those two fields, NOT on the status value — the only guard in this machine, and it does not guard on status. Unchanged at v2.17.2: PR #15085 does not touch payment-module.ts.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply is reject(reason) — an observable no-op. IMPORTANT: the non-authorized branch of authorizePaymentSession is NOT a no-op. It WRITES the provider's status and then raises a MedusaError. The row is persisted; the throw is control flow for the caller. Modelling it as a rejection would falsely imply the state did not change.
trustBound: The provider's reported status is written VERBATIM. packages/modules/payment/src/services/payment-module.ts performs no enum-membership check, no validation, and no mapping — the sole transformation anywhere is authorizePaymentSession_ collapsing CAPTURED into AUTHORIZED. Domain membership is enforced by the DATABASE (the model.enum column), not by the application, so a provider reporting an unknown status fails at write time as a constraint violation rather than being rejected in code. At v2.17.2 this is what makes the new member consequential: pending_authorization is something PROVIDERS emit, into paths that still write verbatim.
projectionBound: These three keys are the ENTIRE observable state. Amounts, currency_code, the provider `data` blob, `context`/idempotency keys, the provider id, and the linked PaymentCollection and Payment entities are OUTSIDE the projection. They are passed to providers but never read to decide a session status. Every guarantee made here is relative to this list.

upstream.migration: packages/modules/payment/src/migrations/Migration20260411223700.ts — widens payment_session_status_check to the seven members. Shipped 2026-04, ~2 months BEFORE the 2026-06 feature commit: the constraint was widened ahead of the code using it.
upstream.moduleServiceUnchanged: packages/modules/payment/src/services/payment-module.ts is NOT in the change commit file list — every status write path is identical to v2.17.1.
